Box #13—Add Something to the Interaction
v1.01 (c) Deutsch & Associates, Inc Published by Marketing Rebel LLC
Idea Power by David L. Deutsch
MarketingRebelClub.com/marketing-rebel-courses/idea-power/
Idea Power / Idea Power Adventures / Adventure #6—Box #13 Add Something to the Interaction
*How might you add an intermediary to the interaction?
How might you add an intermediary to the interaction?
Sometimes, adding something new to the mix is just the catalyst you need to get a whole
different reaction. In advertising, one of my favorite ways to do this is by incorporating
something or someone (like a celebrity) currently in the news. How else might you
accomplish this?
*How might you add the element of feedback to the interaction?
Maybe the problem is that something or someone isn’t getting the necessary feedback.
Customers and potential customers love to give feedback. You should be happy to get it!
But you have to solicit it. Sometimes it’s as simple as asking them for it. Nowadays, of course,
there are many more sophisticated ways to build interaction into an email, social media post,
paid ad, or a landing page. Some of them even give the prospect, such as an interactive quiz
that provides the results.
.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
